subject: Sports
title: The NBA's Three-Point Analytics Revolution
description: A short lesson to help you apply The NBA's Three-Point Analytics Revolution.

hook.text: Math killed the mid-range jumper. Analytics proved that three-pointers and layups are the only efficient shots.

buildup.text: A three-pointer at 35% accuracy yields 1.05 points per shot. A two-pointer at 45% yields only 0.90.

discovery.text: The Houston Rockets' analytics department in 2018 built a team that almost never shot mid-range jumpers.

twist.text: Critics say analytics made basketball predictable. Every team now plays the same way—threes and layups.

climax.text: Mid-range shots dropped from 40% of attempts in 2000 to under 15% by 2023. Math reshaped the sport.

punchline.text: Efficiency beats beauty. Analytics decided which shots are worth taking.

quiz.question: Why did NBA teams stop shooting mid-range jumpers?
options: Analytics showed three-pointers and layups are more efficient per shot
Mid-range shots were banned by new rules
Players could no longer make them accurately
